claims.claim_text: Prometheus can store the operational metrics used by a predictive-operations workflow.
claims.rationale: The Prometheus overview directly states that Prometheus collects and stores metrics as time series data.

claims.claim_text: A Kubernetes CronJob can be used to run forecasts on a repeating schedule, such as hourly.
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes CronJob documentation directly says a CronJob creates Jobs on a repeating schedule.

claims.claim_text: Forecast outputs can be sent to Alertmanager as alerts in a production workflow.
claims.rationale: The Prometheus Alerts API page explicitly documents sending alerts to Alertmanager via `api/v2/alerts`.

claims.claim_text: Predictive maintenance predates software operations and is rooted in physical-asset maintenance, with examples spanning wartime aircraft maintenance and modern manufacturing.
claims.rationale: IBM's predictive-maintenance overview directly discusses the technique's WWII aircraft-maintenance origin and its modern use in manufacturing and transportation sectors.

further_reading.title: Prometheus Docs: Overview
further_reading.why_relevant: Useful primary reference for the time-series storage and alerting model the module uses in its example architecture.

further_reading.title: Kubernetes Docs: CronJob
further_reading.why_relevant: Shows the native Kubernetes scheduling primitive that fits the module's hourly forecast-job example.

further_reading.title: IBM: What Is Predictive Maintenance?
further_reading.why_relevant: Provides concise background on predictive-maintenance history, condition-based monitoring, and why teams act before failure.

notes: Excluded quizzes, exercise scaffolding, code blocks, Mermaid diagrams, and purely illustrative numeric examples unless they made a named-product or historical claim that needs inline support.
